prompt:
The document-index service no longer starts under our framework 5.1 runtime. The settings loader reports `removed setting DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE is defined`; after an operator temporarily bypassed startup validation, `rebuild_index` also failed while importing a text compatibility helper that the new runtime does not expose. The protected migration notes capture the old and current contracts locally.

Please finish the migration across the settings and management-command seams. Preserve the two deployed storage backends and all of their options, the exact middleware order and request effects, the database engine/name/persistent-connection/atomic-request behavior, and the `check` and `rebuild_index` command names. Rebuilds must still be one transaction, roll back partial work, and keep dry-run side-effect free. Do not add a compatibility alias to the protected framework surface or weaken startup validation.

Run `python3 test_django_upgrade.py`. The test, framework contract, migration notes, and ignore file are protected and acceptance is fully offline.

full_distill.primary_skill: interpret protected framework migration notes and update the owning Python settings and management-command boundaries without disturbing adjacent service behavior
full_distill.baseline_evidence: python3 test_django_upgrade.py exits 1 with a deterministic removed-setting failure for DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE and an ImportError because rebuild_index imports the absent force_text API
full_distill.success_contract: the current loader accepts equivalent default and static storage aliases, management commands run through the supported text API, and middleware ordering, database policy, dry-run behavior, transaction commit, and rollback remain unchanged
adversarial_factors: the protected loader rejects either removed storage setting and rejects backend options placed beside BACKEND instead of under OPTIONS, so deleting only the first reported name or flattening configuration does not pass
middleware effects and exact order are asserted separately from settings startup, preventing a wholesale settings rewrite or unordered representation
database engine, path, connection lifetime, and atomic-request flag are pinned while the rebuild command proves one commit, no dry-run mutation, and rollback after a mid-batch rejection
framework51.py is protected and intentionally exposes force_str but not force_text, so restoring a legacy alias in the local contract cannot conceal an unmigrated command
